update_note: Posts are refreshed automatically from trusted .org and .edu sources every two weeks.

posts.title: Ask the Expert: Car Window Tint for UV Protection
posts.summary: Explore the benefits of car window film or tint for UV protection and safeguard your skin from harmful UV rays.

posts.title: Ask the Expert: Pregnancy and Skin Health
posts.summary: There’s lots of information online about pregnancy raising the risk of sunburns. Is that true, or is it misinformation? Our expert sets the record straight!

posts.title: Your June Update: The Truth About Sunscreen
posts.summary: Sunscreen is trending, and with good reason. It’s an important part of a complete sun protection strategy to safeguard your skin from damage that accelerates the signs of aging and increases your skin cancer risk.

posts.title: Dangerous Extremes
posts.summary: Obsession with using products to either lighten or darken the skin can be hazardous to your health.

posts.title: How Much Sunscreen Is Enough?
posts.summary: You may think you’re doing the right thing when it comes to sunscreen. But you're probably not using nearly enough. Check out our new approach on how to apply!

posts.title: Alternatives to Animal Testing for Sunscreen: A Cosmetic Chemist’s Perspective
posts.summary: Backlash to the animal testing required for FDA approval of sunscreen in the U.S. has grown, especially as viable alternatives have emerged. Many, from animal rights activists to members of Congress, are hopeful as the FDA commits to reducing animal testing.

posts.title: We Need Sunscreen Innovation: A Cosmetic Chemist’s Perspective
posts.summary: For a product that has been proven to help prevent skin cancer, sunscreen in the U.S. has had a rough journey lately, from a tidal wave of misinformation to regulatory reroutes and delays. Here, helpful advice — and good news: the FDA approval of the first new sunscreen active…

posts.title: Blitz of Fan Generosity to Tackle Skin Cancer
posts.summary: Fans of Sean McDermott, former coach of the Buffalo Bills, wouldn’t let him stay sidelined for long. They showed their support with a fundraising drive for The Skin Cancer Foundation in his honor. A big win in the fight against skin cancer.

posts.title: Dermatologists Expose the Truth About Skin Cancer
posts.summary: As skin cancer rates continue to rise, our physician members are working with us to address an alarming surge in misinformation online.

posts.title: Your Skin Cancer Awareness Month Update
posts.summary: May is Skin Cancer Awareness Month, the perfect opportunity to share the truth about the dangers of skin cancer and the importance of early detection and prevention. We hope you’ll join us!

posts.title: Sun protection basics
posts.summary: Harvard Health explains why daily broad-spectrum sunscreen, shade, and protective clothing remain the foundation of skin cancer prevention.

posts.title: How to perform a skin self-exam
posts.summary: The American Academy of Dermatology recommends monthly full-body checks to catch changing moles and new spots early.

posts.title: Melanoma awareness and early detection
posts.summary: The Skin Cancer Foundation outlines warning signs and why early dermatologist evaluation improves outcomes.

posts.title: Eczema self-care guidance
posts.summary: Mayo Clinic covers gentle skin care, trigger avoidance, and when moisturizing and medical treatment may help.
